title: Prefer using timezone-aware datetime objects over using datetime.datetime.utcnow() and datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp()
description: Using datetime.datetime.utcnow() and datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp() without considering the timezone can lead to incorrectDateTime handling and confusion.
message: Avoid using datetime.datetime.utcnow() and datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(), use timezone-aware datetime objects instead.
recommendation: Use datetime.datetime.now() or datetime.fromtimestamp() with a timezone.

noncompliant.code:
from datetime import datetime
now_utc = datetime.utcnow()

from datetime import datetime
from_timestamp_utc = datetime.utcfromtimestamp(timestamp)

compliant.code:
from datetime import datetime, timezone
now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from_timestamp = datetime.fromtimestamp(timestamp, timezone.utc)

why_is_this_an_issue: Using datetime.datetime.utcnow() and datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp() without considering the timezone can lead to incorrectDateTime handling and confusion.
how_to_fix: Use datetime.datetime.now() or datetime.fromtimestamp() with a timezone.

metadata.title: Prefer using timezone-aware datetime objects over using datetime.datetime.utcnow() and datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp()
